title: psilotales
intro: The taxonomic rank of Psilotales is an order, which is a classification rank used to group plants with similar characteristics and evolutionary relationships. This order includes a small group of vascular plants, commonly known as whisk ferns, that are found in tropical and subtropical regions. The Psilotales order comprises two families: Psilotaceae and Ophioglossaceae, and is characterized by their unique leafless or scale-like leaves and vascular anatomy. Other examples of plant families that do not belong to the Psilotales order include Orchidaceae, Euphorbiaceae, and Cyperaceae.

families.family_desc: Psilotaceae is a family of vascular plants commonly known as whisk ferns, characterized by their simple, leafless stems and sporangia.
